# KinMate v1.2.0

## Release Date
2026-06-08

## Summary
KinMate v1.2.0 is the first public release — the complete Health Intelligence edition. It is built for the person in every family who ends up holding everyone's health information in their head, their photo album, and scattered chat threads.

If you have ever:
- scrolled through hundreds of photos looking for one lab report
- been asked "what was my test result?" and had to say "let me find it"
- kept medication schedules in notes that nobody else can see
- wanted to understand a report without deciphering medical jargon
- tried to care for parents, kids, or pets while living far away
- needed to hand a doctor your full history on the spot

then KinMate gives you one private place where everything is filed, readable, and ready when someone asks.

KinMate turns scattered health chaos into one organized vault:
- snap or import reports — OCR files them by person, date, and type
- track blood pressure, glucose, lipids, and more with trend charts over 7/30/90 days
- manage medications and gentle reminders for doses, follow-ups, vaccines, and vet visits
- care for parents remotely with daily check-ins and missed-check-in alerts
- share specific profiles with family members without handing over your whole phone
- generate patient summaries and emergency medical cards in seconds
- get AI to explain what's normal, what's not, and what to watch for — never a diagnosis
- back up to your own iCloud, Google Drive, or OneDrive with keys only you hold

What this means in practice:
- answer family health questions in seconds, not minutes
- stop re-explaining the same history to every new doctor
- see what needs attention today without digging through old threads
- understand reports in plain language instead of medical terms
- hand paramedics or physicians the right information immediately
- keep yourself, parents, kids, and pets organized in one app

Why download it now:
- the first 1,000 users get a 60-day full-feature trial instead of the usual 21 days
- Family 8 lifetime founder offer at $199.99 (50% off $399.99) — pay once, keep forever
- this launch ships the full daily workflow — reports, reminders, family care, and Health Intelligence — not a demo shell
- the app and landing page now match, so pricing and the download experience are clear before you install
- feedback is built into settings and sign-in, so you can report issues without leaving the app

This release also includes a unified launch offer across the app and landing site, with a 60-day trial and lifetime Family 8 plan.

## What's New

### Daily care — the foundation
- Private health vault for reports, images, and notes — local-first, searchable by person and date
- Home page status cards for today's check-ins, medications, pet care, and reminders
- Aggregated today view: reminders, medications, care check-ins, and pet tasks in one place
- Medication management with local reminders; AI can suggest adding meds and schedules from reports
- Pet profiles with daily care tasks — feeding, walks, meds, grooming
- Family groups with flexible seat pools — real members or managed profiles for parents and kids
- Care check-in for elderly parents living apart; alert center for missed check-ins and overdue items
- Bring-your-own-cloud backup to iCloud, Google Drive, or OneDrive with end-to-end encryption

### Health Intelligence — new in v1.2
- **AI report interpretation**: structured summary with abnormal and normal indicators shown separately, recovery and follow-up guidance, and optional medication suggestions you can turn into reminders
- **Health trends**: chart key metrics over 7/30/90 days with normal-range markers and AI risk-level summaries when data changes
- **Medical case management**: AI extracts diagnoses and prescriptions from reports into structured cases with timelines
- **Patient summary**: one-tap structured summary (basics, history, medications, allergies, recent labs) ready to share with any doctor
- **Genetic risk analysis**: family history turned into hereditary risk profiles with preventive suggestions
- **Emergency medical card**: QR code with allergies, conditions, medications, and emergency contacts — works offline for first responders
- **Health timeline**: one chronological feed across reports, metrics, cases, medications, vaccines, and pet care logs
- **Family health dashboard**: overview of everyone you care for, with attention-needing items surfaced at the top

### Launch offer
- 60-day launch trial copy on app and site for the first 1,000 users
- Family 3 and Family 8 highlighted as hot plans
- Family 8 lifetime offer shown at $199.99 with $399.99 original price
- Launch promo dialog shown inside subscription pages
- Feedback entry added in settings and sign-in flows

## Changed
- Subscription paywall copy normalized for overseas users
- Site pricing content aligned with the app paywall rules
- Landing page download area shows the signed APK from GitHub Releases
- App version metadata lives under the KinMate app, not the repo root
- App and site release flow stay in sync so users see the same version and download link

## AI Update
- AI report interpretation uses a structured protocol instead of a single summary line
- Normal and abnormal indicators are shown separately; abnormal ones include recovery, follow-up, and warning guidance
- Report text can surface explicit medication suggestions as structured data — add them to the medication system with one tap
- Health trends, genetic risk, and patient summary use cached AI results — recomputed only when your data actually changes
- AI interpretation data syncs across devices with version-aware merging so newer structured fields are preserved
- One-tap language toggle on report interpretation and patient summary (English ↔ Chinese), cached after first use

## Notes
- `Family 3` and `Family 8` are both marked hot in English and Chinese.
- `Family 8` is a lifetime founder offer and should keep the crossed-out original price visible.
- App Store and Google Play are under review; until they go live, install the signed Android APK from GitHub Releases.
- This is the single canonical release note for the first public release.
